tweet: It’s time for Democrats like Sen. Durbin to keep politics out of the post office. We need to give our letter carriers, local post masters, and other USPS employees the resources they need to do their jobs. They shouldn’t be pawns in the Democrats’ partisan games.

tweet: And I’d just like to note - I have been in Congress since 2013. @SenatorDurbin has been in Congress since 1983. What accomplishments from his 37 years in office can he point to that have addressed the long-term solvency problems of the @USPS?

tweet: @TransportGOP @RepSamGraves 5. tweet: @TransportGOP @RepSamGraves 4. Addressing the infrastructure needs of America’s rural communities – 71% of U.S. public road lane-mileage is in rural America. name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: @TransportGOP @RepSamGraves 3. Streamlining the project delivery process to maximize available funding – time is money, and reducing red tape in the project review and delivery process will yield better results for taxpayer investments in infrastructure and save money.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: @TransportGOP @RepSamGraves 2. tweet: Thanks to everyone for tuning in and for asking so many great questions. It's an honor to serve as your representative in Washington.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "We want to make the tax process as simple as possible and allow people to give more to charity at the same time."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Let's continue to work on making our tax code fairer, flatter, and simpler. It's an issue that we have to address to help all Americans."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: The final question tonight comes from Susan in Plainview about tax reform.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "We have a lot of roads that need repair, and we need to make those investments."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Barbara from Staunton has a question regarding infrastructure.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Continue to be an advocate (for bipartisanship). Rep's and Dem's do get along in DC, & most of what we vote on is truly bipartisan."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Vanessa had a questions about encouraging bipartisanship in today's political climate.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I will work with the administration on enforcement of trade deals,but we can't pretend deals like NAFTA haven't been good for our economy."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Trade is crucial to us selling our hometown products overseas. We must have incentives to have global trade opportunities to keep our jobs.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Terri called in with a question about NAFTA and trade issues.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Coal has a valid and appropriate place in our national energy portfolio, and we'll fight to have those opportunities in the future."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "We'd love to see coal and clean coal technology continue be part of our energy portfolio.These are the energy investments we need to make."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Alan from Springfield has a question about coal jobs in Illinois.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I'm proud to have sponsored HR 795, which allows companies to help graduates pay off student loan debt."   https://t.co/kMouR9WjEG

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "We've seen the staggering student loans that many families face. We must do more than just address the cost of college..."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Patrick from Calhoun County has a question about job opportunities for college graduates.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I want those who commit the worst crimes to have the longest sentences. I want people to get the help they need over longer sentences."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Atty General Sessions and I will disagree on the best ways to reform the criminal justice system."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Criminal justice reform is something I've been talking about during my entire tenure in Congress. We must reform our mandatory minimums."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: David from Edwardsville has a question about criminal justice reform.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "We need to ensure we have the educational resources and workforce to ensure these companies can continue to grow."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "We've seen what investment from companies like ADM have done to the Macon County economy."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Alan has a question regarding supporting local jobs.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I hope we get bipartisanship in regard to fixing a broken tax code... I would like to see a revenue neutral tax reform plan."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "We have been stuck in an anemic economic growth rate... I want to see us grow our economy even more."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I believe we need to be able to lower tax rates for middle class Americans and put more money in their pockets & close loopholes."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Pamela from Normal called to ask about tax cuts.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Let's make sure we stop interference, not just in elections, but in any aspect of the US... our economy, cyber attack, & national defense."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Any interference from any country needs to be investigated and stopped. Which is why you are seeing 3 investigations into Russian's role."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: The next caller is Maria, who is asking about foreign influence in elections.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I want to make our public education system stronger. Last Congress we did just that by reforming "No Child Left Behind" on bipartisan vote.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I've never been a fan of vouchers proposed... They would not be beneficial to many of the schools in our district due to logistics issues."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Kenneth called to ask about school funding.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I've always stood up and said I want to support pre-existing conditions."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I've supported a bill that protects those with pre-existing conditions. The bill we passed has multiple layers of protection."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: Evan called in with a question about healthcare and pre-existing conditions.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "The truth is that most bills that pass Congress pass with large bipartisan margins."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I've seen our country come together in the midst of tragedies... If you just watched cable news, you'd think rep's and dem's don't talk."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "I've been ranked as one of the most moderate Congressman. I've got a reputation of working in a bipartisan way."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: The next question comes from Doug in Springfield, who asks about alleviating the division between the political parties.

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "It's a major issue we want to tackle. If you have any ideas on updating the tax code, please pass them along to my office."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "It contains many pages of loopholes that both parties talk about eliminating, and that's what we have the opportunity to do."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: "Taxes are a major issue that we must address. We have a broken tax code that is way too onerous."

name: US Rep Rodney Davis
tweet: The first question of the evening comes from Stanley, who asks about tax relief at the federal level.
